title: Nominate your 2023 Conservation Hero!
summary: It’s officially our favorite time of year as we kick off our search for another incredible cohort of conservation heroes. Do you know an individual or organization that deserves recognition? Nominations will be accepted through April 16, 2023. Awards will be made in the following categories: Lifetim...

title: Challenging travel conditions result in longer wait time for medical travel
summary: In our region, some customers have experienced extended wait times that are beyond our control for travel to medical appointments. In many instances, there are not enough lodging rooms and airline seats available to meet the demand. Amid these limitations, requests for medical travel are up nearly 2...

title: NANA Mourns the Passing of Oliver Aveogan Leavitt
summary: We are deeply saddened to hear of the passing of Oliver Aveogan Leavitt. He was one of our respected Iñupiaq relatives, a neighbor and friend to the NANA region, and one of the giants of Alaska. The results of his life remain. As an umialik — a whaling captain — he led his crew and together they sha...

title: Thank you!
summary: May One Match Day 2023 was a rousing success and it’s all because of your love and support. To all our donors, challengers, and volunteers: thank you, Thank You, THANK YOU! With your help we raised over $112,443 to keep KUAC going strong!

title: First Arctic Policy Hackathon Addresses Food Sovereignty
summary: The first Arctic Policy Hackathon took place in Reykjavík in 2022 and brought together leaders from Arctic Indigenous and local communities. The post First Arctic Policy Hackathon Addresses Food Sovereignty appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .
